agglomerations.unified_desc: Intent agglomeration and refresh lives in agent behavior trained through the intent-maintenance skill, not engine logic (the only supporting code is the relations field). An automated auditor auto-proposes refreshes; the psyche confirms each source-record retire, preserving the conservative over-removal-is-worse discipline.

The auditor is itself an agent, so the arrows are complementary, not conflicting; unified wording preserves both the where-logic-lives claim and the propose-then-human-gate claim. Both records Medium/Minimum, same domain.

negatives.new_desc: Respond to Spirit overcapture by applying the conservative capture gate: use Spirit only for explicit durable decisions, principles, corrections, clarifications, and constraints. Continued disciplined use, not abstinence, is the correction.
negatives.reason: This is a Correction whose load-bearing boundary is that abstinence is the wrong response to overcapture; the reword preserves that boundary while leading with the affirmative discipline.

negatives.new_desc: Reports form a small load-bearing current-state set: refresh even still-relevant reports into current-state form when context maintenance runs, retiring the older form once its substance is carried forward. History lives in jj/git; load-bearing context lives in the current set.
negatives.reason: Leads with the positive small-current-set rule while preserving the retire-after-substance-carried and history-in-vcs arrows.

condense.new_desc: Deployed Spirit can REMOVE intent records, superseding the append-only constraint. Remove two classes: (1) stale records superseded by a newer record whose substance is absorbed in a record, INTENT.md, or skill; (2) working orders that fail the after-the-task test. Removal stays psyche-authorized; when removability is uncertain, flag rather than remove.
condense.reason: Condensed to the operative two-class removal rule plus the conservative flag-when-uncertain arrow; only restatement and over-removal narration stripped.

condense.new_desc: For a multi-lane prompt the default capturer is the lane that responds first (typically the operator/Codex, faster than the designer/Claude). The slower lane gap-checks the capture and gap-fills only a genuine omission rather than writing a parallel entry. Per psyche 2026-06-05.
condense.reason: Strips the war-story rhetoric (recurring mistaken-duplicate pairs, prior backwards draft) while keeping the first-responder-records / slower-lane-gap-checks rule and the dated psyche attribution.

condense.new_desc: Agglomerating repeated or related intent preserves source provenance and may preserve accumulated importance via weight where that axis exists, but does not automatically raise certainty; certainty rises only when the synthesized statement is itself better supported or stated with higher confidence.
condense.reason: Strips the 'record d5s2 overstates' meta-commentary while keeping the operative rule on what agglomeration does and does not change to certainty/importance/provenance.

agglomerations.unified_desc: The cloud daemon starts almost-stateless and holds a runtime, in-memory, lossy cache of last-queried Cloudflare resources; Cloudflare is the source of truth so cache loss is acceptable, and persistent storage is deferred until state worth preserving emerges.
agglomerations.reason: All three express one arrow: cloud cache is volatile/lossy, the provider is source of truth, persistence deferred. g055 and p83x are partial restatements of m3eg. Unified preserves every distinct clause.

agglomerations.unified_desc: Real-time streaming is a new signal capability (working name signal-real-time) carried as a Signal rather than Sema; its storage format stays open, likely a native real-time format rather than Sema.
agglomerations.reason: Both records describe the same signal-real-time capability; vz7c refines lvy9 with the Signal-not-Sema classification and open storage question. Both arrows preserved.

agglomerations.unified_desc: The guardian decides a proposed entry together with the new referents it introduces and the justifying psyche statement as one atomic accept or reject; a refused entry admits no orphan referent, and a self-service synonym check alone never registers a referent.
agglomerations.reason: Both record the same arrow: referents are guardian-gated atomically with their record; kasm states the atomic accept/reject (no orphan referent), ut6z states referents are not self-service. Both arrows preserved in one rule.

agglomerations.unified_desc: Zero certainty IS the Spirit removal-candidate state: such records carry no value and stay hidden by default. Query carries a certainty/magnitude selection with a floor defaulting to Minimum (excludes Zero); review workflows select low-confidence or removal-candidate records by setting an explicit floor (e.g. Zero/Any).
agglomerations.reason: zyab (filter by certainty/magnitude to find removal candidates) is the query mechanism oj3i already specifies via its certainty floor. zyab is fully subsumed; both arrows preserved.

agglomerations.unified_desc: Spirit privacy querying exposes all privacy selectors as operations on an explicit privacy-query subtype carrying the privacy level — at-most-inclusive (the main one), exact-equal, and at-least — plus ergonomic public-only and private-only shortcut verbs; the normal query stays Zero-only.
agglomerations.reason: Both shape Spirit privacy querying: 7vre defines the full selector set on the privacy subtype, 7783 adds the public/private shortcut verbs over it. Tightly-related single arrow; both clauses preserved.

negatives.new_desc: Spirit holds only durable psyche statements (Decision/Principle/Correction/Clarification/Constraint) that outlive any single task and guide future work. Test: would the record still guide AFTER the task it refers to is done? Ephemeral working orders (create-a-report, dispatch-a-subagent, audit-Y, integrate-branches) belong in the session task list or beads; from a task-directive prompt capture only the durable intent within it. A mis-logged working order is flagged as a capture error, not deleted (append-only provenance). Manifest the test in skills/intent-log.md.
negatives.reason: Leads with the affirmative rule (Spirit holds durable statements; the test) and keeps the working-order exclusion as a boundary clause; retains the flagged-not-deleted/append-only arrow and the manifest target; strips the record 1090/1088/1091 narration.

negatives.new_desc: A psyche statement that leans toward a choice while explicitly requesting more context is captured as a lean-pending-information (low certainty), not a ratified Decision. The conservative discipline understates when the psyche has flagged more-information-needed.
negatives.reason: Reframed affirmatively (capture leans as lean-pending-info at low certainty) and condensed; removes the Spirit 1511 war story while keeping the distinguish-and-understate discipline.

Use 'sema database' in all design conversations and reports. Sema holds strings today (verbatim quotes, agent names, summaries), reducing over time as natural language becomes typed leaves.

condense.new_desc: Spirit certainty uses an honest 8-level rubric, assigned deliberately and never reflexively: Maximum for repeatedly-ratified stable foundational invariants and hard overrides; VeryHigh for ratified decisions in force; High for solid decisions with minor open edges; Medium for working decisions and settling design directions (the honest home for most ticks); Low/VeryLow for leans, proposals, and pending-information; Minimum for weak tentative signal; Zero for removal candidates.
condense.reason: Stripped the rhetorical aside about agents historically marking Maximum; kept the full level-by-level rubric and the deliberate-never-reflexively discipline, which are the operative content.

The daemon already stamps date-time. Lets one query mix all four dimensions (e.g. recent records on a topic at a certainty level).

agglomerations.unified_desc: A schema/.asschema file's root struct is implied by its extension and filename (mirroring Rust modules), so the text carries the root's fields positionally with no outer wrapping record. The .schema root's first field is the imports/exports namespace (brace map); subsequent positional fields are the input/output struct(s), combinable into one when both present, optionality applying to sub-fields. The root holds a key-value map of datatype names to raw NOTA datatype objects.
agglomerations.reason: All five express one arrow: a schema file's root comes from file context, so text is positional with no (...) wrapper. 0srq adds field layout, 44gj the datatype-map and filename-derivation, bpg9 the .asschema case. Certainty lowered to Medium (the lowest member, 0srq/bpg9/44gj) since the unified claim asserts their union.

agglomerations.unified_desc: NOTA's bracket-only string discipline means a NOTA expression never contains a double-quote, so it embeds escape-free inside any double-quote host (JSON, Rust incl raw strings, Nix, YAML, TOML, shell, HTTP bodies, DB columns, env vars, XML). This is a load-bearing carrier property: brackets carry NOTA escape-free, while schema is the interpretation mechanism deriving an embedded NOTA's meaning from its embedding site.
agglomerations.reason: 7y8w states the escape-free embedding property; mrcx states the carrier-vs-interpretation split (brackets carry, schema interprets per site). Same arrow plus mrcx's interpretation point, both preserved. Certainty lowered to High (mrcx) from 7y8w's VeryHigh.

agglomerations.unified_desc: Square brackets in NOTA are the raw vector container delimiter, not the schema syntax for declaring a Vec type; declaring or referencing a vector type uses an explicit parenthesized typed object (Vec of X). At a typed schema position where the expected type is String or a string newtype, bracket content is read as string data, so [] is not always semantically a vector.
agglomerations.reason: voa8: brackets are the raw vector delimiter, Vec declared via explicit (Vec of X). 3em3: same brackets read as string at typed String positions. Two halves of one bracket-meaning rule. Certainty lowered to Medium (3em3) from voa8's High.

agglomerations.unified_desc: In NOTA, plain PascalCase tokens are unit variants, and parenthesized variants carry data.
agglomerations.reason: Both are one-line NOTA variant-encoding rules forming a single pair: bare PascalCase = unit variant; parenthesized = data-carrying. Obvious unification with no loss; both High.

agglomerations.unified_desc: NOTA braces { } are the native strict key-value map delimiter: every entry is exactly a key plus a value, never a single token. Schema honors this, reading a brace object as key-value structure (macros may consume these at schema positions). A namespace body is { TypeName TypeDefinition ... }, a struct body is { fieldName TypeRef ... }; derived-name shorthand uses a value-side marker like (Derive). At any brace position the valid keys form a typed enum whose variant (by case and sigil) dispatches the value type.
agglomerations.reason: ghw7 is the full brace=strict-key-value-map decision (namespace/struct body forms, (Derive) value-side marker, typed-KeyEnum dispatch); 1rci restates KV-ness as low-level NOTA structure read by schema, macros consuming at positions. Same arrow. Importance High from ghw7 kept; certainty lowered to Medium (1rci) from ghw7's High.

agglomerations.unified_desc: schema-next needs a Bytes primitive (ideally fixed-size byte arrays) so binary domain values — BLS public keys, signatures, digests, fingerprints, nonces, content identifiers — are typed as bytes not String, beyond the current String/Integer/Boolean/Path primitives. It should also offer a hash-identifier type whose domain value is bytes but whose NOTA projection is a canonical short string code (stored as bytes, read/written as text), generalizing RecordIdentifier (12 bytes as base36).
agglomerations.reason: yp29 asks for a Bytes primitive for binary values; lm84 asks for a bytes-backed hash-identifier with string NOTA projection. Both schema-next binary additions; distinct primitive-vs-projecting-newtype arrows preserved. Both Medium.

agglomerations.unified_desc: A schema header root is a vector of ordered enum-root variant declarations, not a namespace vector.
agglomerations.reason: 9uje: header root is a vector of ordered enum variants. ejum: schema headers are ordered enum-root declarations, not namespace vectors. Identical arrow stated twice; both Medium.

agglomerations.unified_desc: When sketching schemas, use the latest accepted schema/ASSchema syntax; treat older parenthesized variant-list examples and the obsolete syntax shape of reports/operator/226-asschema-first-final-data-2026-05-28.md as stale unless explicitly marked legacy.

condense.new_desc: NOTA structs are positional only — no field names in the wire form; an object's position within a struct, via the schema applied at read time, determines what it is. Head tags on schema fields are unnecessary because position already encodes meaning.
condense.reason: Strips the Designer-326-v2 war story while keeping the positional-only rule and the no-redundant-tags point.

condense.new_desc: In NOTA, inline a data-carrying variant's payload as a bracket list at the variant position when the enum is single-use — referenced only here, small (2-6 variants), no independent identity, name already carries the meaning. Declare a named enum type separately when it is reused across positions, has independent semantic identity, is large (10+ variants), or is part of a stable contract.
condense.reason: Keeps the inline-vs-named decision rule and its criteria; strips the worked Busy example and the cross-record provenance citations (1466/1411/1387/nota-design.md).

Merged into the ta58+0zci agglomeration as a named instance of the stale-syntax rule; retire to keep lineage visible since it is genuinely subsumed by the merge.

agglomerations.unified_desc: Sema-upgrade is a new persona-system component: the universal stateful schema-upgrade mechanism for sema databases (the typed-record stores in persona components, today backed by redb). Every persona daemon that needs to upgrade goes through it. Its first test is the legacy intent log to spirit migration: treat legacy intent/*.nota as a 0.01 spirit database and translate it to spirit's current schema. Eventually the schema-spec-language drives its transformations from declarative schema diffs.

Assembled schemas may be stored to enable efficient diffing.
condense.reason: Trimmed the numbered mini-essay and the 'next/main/previous vocab per rec 672' narration to the terse operative arrow, but RESTORED the assembled-schema-for-efficient-diffing aside, which is a distinct (tentative) design arrow the first pass dropped.

agglomerations.unified_desc: The component top-level schema for the Signal/Nexus/Sema planes is the REACTIVE SCHEMA; the simpler BASE SCHEMA is pure data specification (a root struct plus its namespace of type definitions). Base schema says what data IS; reactive schema extends it with the reaction surface (signal in, nexus through, sema state, output back) saying how data MOVES. Most schemas are plain base; a component top-level schema is reactive. Analogy: a Nix derivation is the built-in primitive that higher builders extend. Syntax details (namespace ordering, base rooting, field-name derivation) remain open.
agglomerations.reason: Both define the same reactive-schema vs base-schema distinction with the identical Nix-derivation analogy; xzlv adds the in/through/state/out mapping and the IS-vs-MOVES framing, mimk adds the open-syntax items; unified preserves both.

agglomerations.unified_desc: The Spirit removal-candidate floor is a bottom VARIANT named Zero on the shared signal-sema Magnitude vocabulary — a neutral always-present rung declared first so derived Ord places it below Minimum, chosen for policy-neutrality and to avoid Option/None confusion. Magnitude stays a bare always-present field. Removal-candidate meaning is persona-spirit's per-component interpretation of certainty equals Zero, layered on the shared payload-free vocabulary (persona-mind could read the same rung as forgettable).
agglomerations.reason: Both settle the same Zero bottom-rung Magnitude decision for removal candidates; oxy1 is the fuller settled form of dnvs's proposal, including the persona-mind reading and Ord-first ordering.

agglomerations.unified_desc: Schema files carry structure only; explanatory prose lives in the Rust implementation files. Schema declarations are positional and self-explanatory by the names of the types they declare, so the schema source itself stays comment-free.
agglomerations.reason: mkts is a strict subset of bw9v — both say schema files carry structure not comments; merge into the fuller affirmative form.

agglomerations.unified_desc: The assembled schema represents a struct as a key-value brace map: each field key is the field name and each field value is the type reference. This is the lowest-level long-form data object that authored struct sugar expands into.
agglomerations.reason: oxgh and 5mbd state the identical decision (struct as key-value brace map = expanded struct sugar) at the same altitude.

agglomerations.unified_desc: A schema declares its roots at one designated root position; nesting adds no further roots. Roots are the named entry-point set, generalizing the fixed input/output pair into a roots collection that matches the operator roots model. Everything else is a free datatype declared in the namespace and referenced by roots at some position.
agglomerations.reason: p02v is an explicit gap-fill restating i8wt's roots-at-one-point / free-datatype model, including the operator-roots-model alignment; same arrow.

agglomerations.unified_desc: A schema root header is a bare list of PascalCase exported object names for the Input and Output roots; each bare name resolves to a real named type defined in the namespace or inline, serving as both the operation/reply vocabulary and an exported top-level type downstream crates import. The variant payload shape resolves from the namespace rather than repeating a parenthesized pair per variant; reports and docs should stop presenting the parenthesized-signature form.
agglomerations.reason: All three say the root header is bare exported names with payload resolved from the namespace, not parenthesized per-variant pairs; unified now also preserves hrte's distinct corrective that docs/reports stop showing the parenthesized form.

agglomerations.unified_desc: The schema header declaration is a vector of ordered enum variants forming enum namespace nodes, not one payload pair per verb.
agglomerations.reason: uqoi (header is a vector of ordered enum variants) and wipc (headers are enum namespace nodes, not one payload pair per verb) state the same shape.

agglomerations.unified_desc: Spirit record identifiers should be stable, non-reusable durable handles, not reusable incremental numbers — removing a record must not free its id for a later capture (a Principle capture once took freed slot 2572), so citations and links treating the id as durable stay valid. Unique hashes are the recommended identity direction; recency tracking uses daemon-stamped time, separate from identity.

Unified states hashes as the recommended direction without falsely closing 853n's recorded hash-vs-counter openness, and keeps the distinct content-address rejection in separate record kr1v.

negatives.new_desc: Schema declares data types only. Effects are runtime dispatch/logic and live in the runtime, not the schema. Schema variants stay limited to data-type declarations — no effect tables, fan-out targets, or storage descriptors (the EffectTable/FanOutTargets/StorageDescriptor Feature variants were never psyche-authorized).

condense.new_desc: Reactive and base schemas are purely positional: Input and Output enums are written bare at their known positions, never labeled, because the type already knows which position is which. Unifying rule: a name appears only where something is genuinely keyed (a namespace map entry by its key, a sum variant by its tag); a positional slot (struct fields, the input/output positions) is bare because position already says what it is.

condense.new_desc: Define the ASSEMBLED schema first: the canonical, macro-free, final data model Rust is generated from. In it, vector/optional/map/enum/struct/newtype are final forms — variants of the assembled type-model sums (type-reference: Plain, Vector, Map, Optional; type-declaration: Struct, Enum, Newtype). Surface sugar is decided only after the assembled target is locked, never invented first.

condense.new_desc: Universal Magnitude type replaces per-component small-vocabulary ordinal enums (including Certainty), living in the shared typed-record crate (signal-core). Seven variants: Minimum, VeryLow, Low, Medium, High, VeryHigh, Maximum. Variant count is binary-free under rkyv's fixed-byte discriminator; components action only the subset they care about. Name may wrap (SizeMagnitude) or stay Magnitude. Supersedes the narrow Certainty enum in signal-persona-spirit.

condense.new_desc: The workspace Rust subset and schema language handle only named-field structs, named-variant enums (bare, data-carrying, struct-variant, nested), single-field newtype structs, and scalar primitives. Tuples are excluded (treated as poorly-defined structs) — no multi-field tuple structs, anonymous tuples, or field-less anonymous types. The NOTA grammar enforces this at the user layer; the schema-source layer makes it explicit.

Reuse existing topic words when they cover the substance; invent a new word only when none fits.

Adopt branch/leaf vocabulary in design and skill files.

agglomerations.unified_desc: Component Sema databases (daemon durable state) must back up atomically to a server with no state loss; pursue native version-controlled component DBs over opaque blobs, solving the strict-typed hard-migration-per-schema-change shape (Dolt-informed). Build it once as a reusable library of generic types and traits every component can opt into; the exact generic mechanism is undesigned, to be settled by prior-art research and implementation experiments.
agglomerations.reason: j487 explicitly 'Extends 29pb' and restates the same atomic-durable version-controlled Sema database goal, adding only the build-once reusable-single-library requirement and the opt-in option. Unified preserves every arrow: atomic backup, no state loss, version-controlled-over-blobs, Dolt, hard-migration shape, one shared library, per-component opt-in, research-settled mechanism. Stronger 29pb certainty/importance governs since j487 extends it.

agglomerations.unified_desc: Spirit supports removal-candidate review as a first-class observation flow keyed on record certainty or magnitude: a collect-removal-candidates operation surfaces low-confidence (Zero-certainty) records and archives or emits them for review before they are retracted from the hot store.
agglomerations.reason: Both describe one removal-candidate review flow: surface low-confidence records by certainty/magnitude, review and archive/emit before removal rather than hard-delete. 4wlk is the concrete collect-removal-candidates operation realizing mtrx's first-class flow; no distinct arrow lost.

agglomerations.unified_desc: Schema upgrade is live typed SEMA operations on the Asschema: protocol or DB-format changes are isolated as typed upgrade objects, applied as database work, and used as the source for derived datatype plus upgrade/compatibility code, so new generated components accept previous schema/data/message shapes through explicit typed upgrade paths. One SEMA library handles record-level and schema-changing operations — the same kind of operation on different objects.
agglomerations.reason: All four state the same core arrow: schema upgrades are typed SEMA operations that drive generated upgrade/compatibility code. py4h (live SEMA ops as DB work, source of derived datatype+upgrade code), 1wn5 (SEMA ops on Asschema, same library record-level+schema-changing), wv7w (diffs isolated as typed upgrade ops), z550 (typed upgrade objects emit compatibility code accepting previous shapes) collapse into one principle; each distinct arrow is retained in the unified text.

agglomerations.unified_desc: Correcting reports/designer/261: NOTA's strict positional rule governs the user-facing text format only; Cap'n-Proto-style structural-compatibility discipline lives at the rkyv storage layer, whose natural layout reserves headroom (a bool occupies a byte; a small-variant enum discriminator fits in a byte). That headroom enables zero-cost no-migration changes — adding a unit variant still fitting the discriminator byte, appending struct fields under append-only encoding, widening fixed-width ints within reserved space — provided variant order is preserved so existing payloads still decode. The schema-layout schema must expose this headroom (e.g. declared discriminator width) so migration-derivation can identify and skip zero-cost changes.
agglomerations.reason: gvgu (Correction rebutting reports/designer/261's claim that NOTA-no-tail-omission removes Cap'n-Proto field-id discipline, locating that discipline at the rkyv layer with the byte-headroom example) and ux4w (the same byte-headroom zero-cost-change class plus the schema-layout-exposes-headroom requirement) describe one concept. Kept kind=Correction (not the first-pass Principle) to preserve gvgu's load-bearing corrective arrow that the 261 claim was wrong; gvgu's High certainty governs. Over-merge note: this is a tight conceptual match, not mere topical adjacency.

condense.new_desc: Components have EXTERNAL and INTERNAL schemas. EXTERNAL covers surfaces crossing daemon-runtime boundaries: wire contracts to other components (signal vocabulary surviving the socket) and database/storage contracts (state surviving process exit). INTERNAL covers in-runtime surfaces: actor message vocabularies and inter-actor channel contracts sharing process memory. Multiple schemas per component, each describing one channel. Internal-schema changes leave wire and storage formats intact; major actors get a schema for their message vocabulary.
condense.reason: Strips the meta-commentary about 'refining record 659' and the two-languages-now-wider-taxonomy narration; keeps the EXTERNAL/INTERNAL taxonomy, the per-channel rule, the internal-changes-don't-break-external-surfaces arrow, and the major-actors-get-a-schema rule.

condense.new_desc: Authoring vocabulary, workspace-wide: NEXT = the in-progress version being authored (authors always write from the point of view of next); MAIN = the current published canonical baseline imported as the comparison point; PREVIOUS/LAST = the prior iteration of any contract, schema, message format, or data shape. If main == next at the same revision, no upgrade path is generated; if main != next, the upgrade machinery generates the From-chain version-projection from main to next.
condense.reason: Mini-essay with heavy all-caps restatement and spirit-cli.md historical-naming narration. Condensed to the operative vocabulary (NEXT/MAIN/PREVIOUS/LAST), the workspace-wide scope, and the main==next development-model rule with the From-chain emission.

condense.new_desc: Each persona contract carries an explicit NOTA-based schema-layout schema that is content-addressable: its hash is its identity and version, so any edit changes the address. The version-checking pipeline detects schema-address mismatches between code and stored data, walks the diff between addresses, and derives migration operations (zero-cost variant extensions, append-only fields, structural migrations) from it. Migration is baked into how types are written rather than authored separately.
condense.reason: Trims 'refining intent record 12', 'More sophisticated than Cap'n Proto', and 'warrants deep testing before committing' rhetoric; keeps the content-addressable schema-layout, hash-is-version, address-diff migration-derivation, and migration-baked-into-type-writing arrows.

condense.new_desc: The schema component has three faces from one NOTA schema source: (1) a runtime daemon serving short-header-to-schema lookups; (2) a library crate exposing schema types other crates import to reference schema metadata; (3) a macro substrate the signal-side and symmetric sema-side macros consume to generate code from NOTA schema input. The library is the build-time face; the daemon is the runtime face.
condense.reason: Removes the spirit-390/391 citations and the repeated restatement of the three faces; keeps the three-faces decision plus the build-time/runtime split and the single-NOTA-source arrow.

condense.new_desc: The sema-upgrade mechanism is a structural prerequisite for any deployed persona component: the deploy-restart-update flow means a daemon coming up against an existing redb must find either no schema drift or a migration path through sema-upgrade. For mind and orchestrate deployment, either their schema is stable, or sema-upgrade covers their migration before deployment, or the operator drops-and-rebuilds on every contract edit until sema-upgrade catches them.
condense.reason: Tightens the 'without that path, any edit breaks the next restart' restatement while keeping the prerequisite arrow, the no-drift-or-migration-path requirement, and the concrete mind/orchestrate stable-or-covered-or-drop-and-rebuild implication.

condense.new_desc: Persona daemon schema migration uses in-process versioned reads: every stored record carries a schema-version tag, the daemon dispatches read-side on the tag and migrates older records into the current shape on read (optionally rewriting). No external migrator binary; zero downtime per schema bump. The daemon links every prior schema version's types and read paths fork by version (collapsible later). Applies to every persona triad. Per-record versioning, not per-table or per-database.
condense.reason: Strips the quoted selection-wording justification at the end; keeps the per-record versioned-read migration decision, the no-external-migrator and zero-downtime properties, the linked-prior-types/forked-paths trade-offs, every-persona-triad scope, and the per-record (not per-table/per-database) granularity.

removals.reason: Low-importance generic restatement that schema is the macro-language source of truth for component data/wire/storage/upgrade; the operation-source arrow is covered more specifically by 5hjv (operations declared in schema first, generated types are the source) and the macro-substrate framing by z8gj. Nominate (recoverable) since the 'macro-language source of truth' phrasing is mildly distinct but Minimum-importance and well-subsumed.

'Version migration is per type' is a four-word fragment; the per-type granularity is carried explicitly by 5cyn (changed TYPES require hand-written upgrade, unchanged TYPES emit none) and realized at runtime by w1ss's per-record versioned reads. Minimum importance, no distinct surviving arrow. Nominate (recoverable) rather than hard remove.

agglomerations.unified_desc: Schema-emitted Rust types derive NOTA and rkyv traits from the same data objects and sit on the same NOTA typed interfaces direct Rust code uses; reusing the NOTA decode/encode traits beats emitting a parallel mini-codec per schema. Additional communication traits belong on generated schema root objects that cross component boundaries.
agglomerations.reason: Both say schema-emitted types should reuse the same NOTA/rkyv typed traits rather than a parallel codec. wiq2 adds the cross-boundary communication-traits clause, which the unified wording preserves. Both arrows kept.

agglomerations.unified_desc: Across the schema/NOTA stack, source text and the checked-in serialized artifact share one typed noun when they represent the same object, unless a real semantic projection justifies splitting them. Concretely a SchemaMacro record fits one serializable enum variant covering both source and data, not a separate source enum and data enum.
agglomerations.reason: qat2 (macro library source = artifact, one datatype) is the macro-library instance of rmb8's general rule (don't split one semantic object into source vs serialized datatypes). Unified keeps the general rule and the concrete SchemaMacro instance.

agglomerations.unified_desc: Short-header sub-enums pre-allocate encoding space by inner-type semantics, each taking the minimum bits its variant set needs: Bool 1 bit, Option 2 bits (None vs Some-with-inner-tag), a 16-variant enum 4 bits; freed bits encode deeper variant nesting, and a sub-enum over 256 variants spans multiple byte positions. Only enums (incl Bool) qualify; integers are typed values. This lets SEMA pack smaller than raw rkyv enums within 64 bits.
agglomerations.reason: Both express pre-allocating enum/header space by inner-type semantics so each enum takes minimum bits (Bool 1, Option 2), integers excluded, SEMA packs smaller than rkyv. mvmu adds multi-byte and freed-bits detail; o3kn adds Option and the SEMA-vs-rkyv framing. Unified keeps every arrow.

negatives.new_desc: The assembled schema (.asschema) is canonical NOTA and rkyv only: the round-trip form produced by AsschemaNotaWriter and parsed by AsschemaNotaReader. Everything is data; an assembled schema has exactly these two representations. Any hand-rolled human-readable line format, even as a so-called readable witness and whether named .asschema or .witness.txt, is deleted rather than renamed (applies record 1109 to the assembled schema).
negatives.reason: Forbidden-format constraint stated with maximum-force rhetoric. negatives.new_desc: NOTA output comes from the typed codec surface: values derive or implement the codec honestly, and a printed type label is a real decodable type shape produced by the codec. Manual string formatting that imitates a NOTA type is not the codec.
negatives.reason: Leads with a prohibition (manual string formatting forbidden). Reworded to lead with the affirmative rule that NOTA output must come from the typed codec, label must be a real decodable shape. Boundary preserved.

negatives.new_desc: Known-root NOTA serialization uses an object/body parsing-and-encoding abstraction that reads and writes the ordered fields of the known type: a file read as a known root type is already the root body, so the NOTA layer handles its fields through that abstraction rather than ad hoc per-object field string joins.
negatives.reason: Operative guidance is 'must not be ad hoc field string joins'. Reworded to lead with the affirmative: known-root serialization goes through the object/body codec abstraction over ordered fields. Boundary preserved as trailing clause.

negatives.new_desc: The signal layer (signal-frame and the signal-component / meta-signal-component contract crates) carries binary rkyv only; its request/reply types hold no NOTA encoding or projection. NOTA is a separate text-edge concern living outside signal. Supersedes the nota-text-on-signal projection pattern.
negatives.reason: Title clause 'Signal must be NOTA-free' is definition-by-negation. Reworded to lead with the affirmative: signal carries binary rkyv only, NOTA lives outside signal at the text edge. Supersede clause kept.

condense.new_desc: rkyv binary is the single encoded representation living in both the database (sema body at rest) and on the wire (signal movement). One byte layout, two homes; the same AssembledSchema reads bytes in both contexts. NOTA is the text-readable projection emitted at CLI read time or for human inspection.
condense.reason: Condensed restatement and repetition (one byte layout / same encoded form survives storage and transport) into the terse arrow.

condense.new_desc: rkyv is the universal wire base for the schema-derived stack; the NOTA codec (NotaDecode/NotaEncode + derive) is opt-in per consumer. Double clients (text+binary, e.g. CLI) carry the NOTA derive; binary-only consumers (the daemon) compile without it, for leanness and so the daemon's wire structurally cannot accept NOTA text. Same data types, identical rkyv layout; differs only in derive set. The schema-rust-next emitter must produce both forms from one .asschema. Mechanism: cargo feature on the contract crate plus structural split of binary crates (report 430).
condense.reason: Mini-essay with parenthetical justifications and dated provenance condensed to the operative arrows. Restored the dropped 'emitter must produce both forms from one .asschema' clause (a distinct requirement, not mere rhetoric).

condense.new_desc: Canonical name for the 64-bit per-message prefix is short header (or 64-bit short header). Every message carries it; everything else is body. Structure: 8 enums (1 root + 7 sub) occupying the 64 bits, carrying discriminator info only — variant payload lives in the body. Version is enforced at the database level (one schema version per database), not in the header.
condense.reason: Carries name-replacement war-story (Tier 1 micro, small message, small object) and time-indexed-version aside. Condensed to the canonical name, structure, discriminator-only rule, and database-level versioning.

condense.new_desc: Input and output are two partitions of one wire-level enum tag space, not two independent enums: the schema author declares Input and Output separately but the rkyv-compiled form merges them into one tag namespace split by range (e.g. a 1-byte ~240-slot enum roughly two-thirds: low slots Input, high slots Output, illustrative not locked). A single tag byte identifies both the variant and the input/output direction, the basis for direction dispatch at the message header.
condense.reason: Long restatement with the same point made several ways. Condensed to the partition-of-one-tag-space arrow plus the illustrative split and direction-dispatch consequence.

condense.new_desc: Three-tier signal type sizing: (1) 64-bit micro-message — default auto-loggable variant every Signal Operation/Reply/Effect/SemaObservation gets via macro, packing root tag plus sub-discriminators into a u64; (2) 64-byte summary message — hand-implemented when a type has natural summary fields, basis for a second subscription tier; (3) full unrestricted rkyv-encoded message. Variant always at the root; macro autogens tier 1.

condense.new_desc: NOTA mirrors the binary self-describing root-plus-relative-pointer-box layout: the root stays compact with sized fields inline, while unsized/growing fields (String, Vec, Option-of-unsized, nested growth) become boxes appearing after the root in declaration order — box order matches declaration order so no box-index naming is needed. Coordinate notation (vector-N element-M) reads the M-th element of the N-th box vector. The text form is a direct projection of the rkyv-style layout.
condense.reason: Trailing 'second-designer was wondering / psyche ratifies' provenance narration removed; kept the layout-projection arrow and coordinate notation.

condense.new_desc: Identifier design treats the shortest reliable identifier as a first-class target — short readable locators over full hashes — because opaque content-addressed identifiers (blake3/sha) cost ~one LLM token per character wherever they land in agent context, logs, and reports. The full digest stays canonical identity; the exposed locator is a stable shortened form, kept fixed once exposed, balanced against collision risk.
condense.reason: Condensed the cost-explanation preamble; kept the design rule (short locator first-class, full digest canonical, locator stable once exposed, collision tradeoff).

condense.new_desc: Spirit operations support a simpler-to-more-complex variant ladder: short forms with summary defaults for common operations, complex forms with full metadata for precise control. Both coexist in the wire vocabulary as distinct operation roots; the complex root is canonical, the short root expands to a default form of it.
condense.reason: Repeats the daily-use vs precise-control rationale twice; condensed to the ladder definition and the canonical-complex / short-expands arrow.

agglomerations.unified_desc: Cloud's Plan operation is renamed Mutate (request issued to the provider, not yet acknowledged — a pending state) and its acknowledged-state reply is renamed Mutated (provider-confirmed). The state the daemon holds is last-known-acknowledgment, not necessarily what the provider would report if queried right now.
agglomerations.reason: p6j5 (Decision) states the Plan->Mutate/Mutated rename; 8fe9 (Principle) states what each state means and that held state is last-known-acknowledgment. Same Mutate/Mutated naming and pending-vs-acknowledged distinction; one affirmative arrow preserves both the rename and the state semantics.

agglomerations.unified_desc: domain-criome is the authority for .criome domains, giving the workspace its own content-addressed DNS: each .criome domain (e.g. goldragon.criome) is its own content-addressable authority server, and current authority is found by asking the domain's own daemon (last snapshot serves as cached state). A daemon resolves domains it does not own by caching last-known records (content-addressed, timestamped, agreed across Criome nodes); the Criome daemon decides which nodes own which domain, and each daemon may host several domains. NotAuthoritative(Delegation) redirect is the fallback for genuinely-off-network domains where caching has nothing.
agglomerations.reason: ty24 states authority / content-addressed-DNS / ask-the-domain's-own-daemon / last-snapshot caching (networking framing); crlc states the caching / ownership-decision / multi-domain-hosting / delegation-fallback mechanics (replication framing). Same arrow split across two records; unified wording preserves every distinct clause and keeps ty24's networking domain.

agglomerations.unified_desc: Across next/main version divergence, main preserves caller intent: under partial-support, next does the whole operation and main records the divergence (handled the same as cant-do-at-all); when next catastrophically fails, main recovers what it can from the original message via partial application.
agglomerations.reason: Both handle the next/main divergence recovery story — n1fk the partial-support divergence-recording (with its '(same as cant-do-at-all)' qualifier kept attached to the divergence-recording clause, not moved to the catastrophic-failure path), i1jw the catastrophic-failure partial-application recovery. Same fault-tolerance arrow; corrected wording loses neither.

agglomerations.unified_desc: Public-traffic routing adopts /155 Part 2 Design D (Persona-orchestrated FD handoff via SCM_RIGHTS) for lossless cutover: Persona binds one stable public socket per component, accepts client connections, and sends accepted FDs via SCM_RIGHTS to the active-version daemon over a per-component control connection; the client connects to one stable socket and Persona decides which unit listens. Same socket model in dev and prod, with Persona off the byte path after handoff. This supersedes the /154 §2.5 designer recommendation (Design B prod + Design C client-side-discovery dev fallback): client-side discovery (Design C) is rejected in both dev and prod.
agglomerations.reason: n2te (Correction) rejects Design C and supersedes the /154 §2.5 designer recommendation, fixing the one-stable-socket / Persona-routes / no-dev-asymmetry principle; ns7t (Decision) adopts the concrete /155 Design D FD-handoff mechanism that realizes it. The rejection and adoption halves of one routing decision; the unified now explicitly preserves n2te's /154 §2.5 supersession lineage and the Design B/C reference that the first pass had dropped.

negatives.new_desc: Nexus output is slim: input is a query/decision type, output is a result/what-happened acknowledgement, and clients issue follow-up queries for specific state. The wire contract stays compact and identifier-shaped most of the time; the detail lives in the engine. Extends the Spirit 1351 signal slim-acknowledgement pattern up to the Nexus interface level.
negatives.reason: Operative guidance was framed as a prohibition (Don't put everything in every response). Reworded to lead with the affirmative slim-output rule; the avoided case is fully carried by 'clients issue follow-up queries for specific state', so the negative framing is dropped without losing the boundary.

condense.new_desc: Every message carries an ORIGIN ROUTE as implicit metadata (not schema-declared): a return-address that travels through signal then nexus then sema and back, associating a reply with its originating query. It is a short statistically-unique identifier internal to each component, not necessarily a long hash. Refines message-identity records 930 960 by making the correlation identity an implicit round-trip return-address field rather than a schema-declared field.
condense.reason: Trimmed the 'effectively no clashes / just a return address echoed back' rhetorical repetition; kept the operative arrow, the internal-per-component qualifier, and the refines-930-960 lineage.

condense.new_desc: Communicate is a TRAIT — the wire interface between any two components (e.g. CLI and daemon) — implemented over binary rkyv with the signal-frame mechanism (connection setup, async unique IDs for request-reply correlation, handshake). Every component also runs a universal MAIL state manager queuing messages sender-to-daemon; queue admission commits intent to process (as-good-as-fed-in), and the reply confirms processing and carries a DATABASE MARKER (hash plus counter) so the client verifies which transaction the response matches, giving a strong local-state guarantee tied to the daemon's authoritative database.
condense.reason: Condensed the repeated emphasis (async-unique-ID-plus-database-marker together support full async messaging, restated twice) while preserving every distinct mechanism: trait, rkyv/signal-frame, mail queue, as-good-as-fed-in, database marker, local-state guarantee.

condense.new_desc: The signal-nexus-sema order is type-enforced by two mechanisms together: (1) push methods thread the next engine(s) as generic parameters (SignalEngine::push_to_nexus names NexusEngine and SemaEngine; NexusEngine::receive_pushed_signal names SemaEngine); (2) an AcceptedMail token whose only constructor lives inside the SignalActor validation gate is the sole argument the nexus method accepts. Together SEMA is reachable only through Nexus and Nexus only through Signal, with no procedural bypass; the reverse path is return-value threading. The three trait-ordered engines are the sole live path (record 1030).
condense.reason: Stripped the spirit-next 408 prototype framing and the deletion war-story (old procedural handle orchestration, SignalAccepted/FromMail/process_with, vestigial execute passthrough deleted) while keeping both type-enforcement mechanisms verbatim, the reverse-path note, and the 1030 lineage.

condense.new_desc: The lojix deploy daemon serves multiple connections concurrently and does not block while a deploy nix build runs (the driving host dispatches many deploys at once, e.g. updating several hosts in parallel). Engine in-flight deploy state is PER-REQUEST — each connection owns its deploy pipeline cursor — not a shared slot; the durable Store is the shared concurrency point, locked only briefly per sema operation, with long-running nix effects holding no global lock. Concurrency is bounded by a connection permit cap.
condense.reason: Condensed the audit-29 open-question narration (resolves the audit 29 open question decisively against the single-writer serial model) into the operative concurrent-worker decision; kept per-request state, brief-lock Store, no-global-lock-on-nix, and permit-cap arrows.

condense.new_desc: Component state across the Criome stack is last-known-acknowledgment, not live-query: components in a state relationship constantly agree via natural acknowledgment whenever state changes, forming a quorum-of-agreement. For external non-Criome systems (current cloud providers) the local state is the last acknowledgment received until they speak the protocol; future federation enables cross-stack last-known-state status with non-Criome providers.
condense.reason: Trimmed the repeated 'this is the root pattern at Criome — components share state by always agreeing' restatement; kept the principle and the external-provider/federation arrows.

agglomerations.unified_desc: The daemon has three execution centers whose traits encode the order of flow: Signal (communication) processes the incoming signal and pushes to Nexus; Nexus is the mail keeper and translator (mail held by Nexus is in the being-processed state) that gates the signal and translates it to a SEMA query; SEMA (state) runs and produces a state change plus a SEMA reply; then the flow reverses — Nexus translates the SEMA reply (carrying the durable-receipt marker) back into a Signal response and Signal emits it. Push happens only on success: an invalid message is rejected to the client directly; a valid signal proceeds to Nexus, and only an accepted signal proceeds to SEMA. Push methods take the next engine as a parameter so trait signatures themselves sequence Signal then Nexus then SEMA then back. UI and external-IO uses of Nexus are specific uses of its fundamental in-between translator and mail-keeper role.
agglomerations.reason: Both records describe the identical Signal->Nexus->SEMA->reversal flow with trait-encoded ordering and push-only-on-success. o8x5 names the three execution centers and the mail-keeper/translator role; g3f2 names the three engines and parameter-passing trait sequencing. Unified text restores o8x5's distinct arrow that UI/external-IO are specific uses of the fundamental translator role. Certainty floored to Medium because o8x5 (Medium) supplies the mail-keeper/being-processed semantics; the on_sent-hook handoff arrow is carried by the q73w/kgl2 merge.

agglomerations.unified_desc: Message-sent is a hookable event surface: the mail dispatch system emits and commits a typed MessageSent action the moment a message is sent (a method-on-message-sent fires immediately), so UI, observers, routers, and subscribers can react at the message-sent boundary.
agglomerations.reason: q73w (Decision, High) and kgl2 (Principle, Medium) both state the same arrow: message-sent fires a typed event/action immediately so hooks/UI/observers/routers/subscribers react at that boundary. q73w independently asserts it at High; kgl2 adds the typed MessageSent action name and routers. No distinct decision is lost.

agglomerations.unified_desc: Every actor declares its own schema (a channel-contract) with two schema-created base enums: ACTION (what the actor can do when asked) and RESPONSE/reaction (what it can say in reply), plus a universal Unknown response so any unrecognized request gets an answer. Execution matches those schema variants to select replies and route work — same schema substrate as messaging, CLI, and storage, applied per actor.
agglomerations.reason: 8y24 (High) and gmlv (Medium) both describe the per-actor schema declaring action and response/reaction enums. 8y24 adds the Unknown variant and shared-substrate note; gmlv adds the distinct arrow that execution matches schema variants to select replies and route work, which the unified text preserves. Minimum-importance pair; the dominant per-actor-schema claim is High.

negatives.new_desc: The schema declares a closed EFFECT TABLE: a structural mapping from message types to effect types, with the set of possible replies declared per message. Dispatch is match-driven through the interact-trait contact point: incoming message -> mapped effect -> mapped reply. Effects, messaging, and interactions are all mapped; internal work composes these mapped layers. Match always; map always.
negatives.reason: Operative rule is affirmative (the closed effect table and match-driven dispatch through the interact-trait contact point), but the original piles on negations (no ad-hoc dispatch, no string routing, no boolean-flag selection, never compute when you can match). Reword leads with the positive mapping rule and keeps the closed-mapping boundary.

negatives.new_desc: Signal is the first gate: a message is validated at the Signal layer, and only valid, acceptable messages proceed to Nexus and SEMA — an unacceptable message returns a rejection to the client at the Signal boundary.
negatives.reason: Defined primarily by negation (invalid messages do not reach Nexus or SEMA). Reword centers the positive gate rule — valid messages proceed, rejection returns at Signal — while preserving the boundary.

condense.new_desc: Nexus has a typed decision/effect language for non-SEMA decisions: NexusDecision wraps Signal/SemaWrite/SemaRead/Effect, and NexusEffect carries side-channel variants like Stash, Fanout, Summarize, Drop, Enqueue, Preempt, Cascade. The generated runner loops: Nexus decides, the runner executes SEMA and effect actions, completions re-enter Nexus, and only NexusDecision::Signal(Output) exits to the wire. The language's existence is settled; the exact variant list and implementation cost remain designer-side proposals pending evidence.
condense.reason: Strips the 'Refines designer 476 per operator's correction' provenance and the 'without pretending every side effect is already a Signal reply' rhetoric; keeps the typed decision/effect language arrow, the full variant list, the runner loop, the single wire exit, and the existence-settled/variant-list-pending split.

condense.new_desc: message owns the EXISTENCE fact as a durable event it emits — that an authenticated message was authored and witnessed at the SO_PEERCRED ingress boundary — distinct from the router DELIVERY fact (durable on harness-side ack). message is feature-bearing with its own state surface, not a stateless pass-through. message and router stay SEPARATE components because the SO_PEERCRED trust boundary cannot move into router.
condense.reason: Strips the report-provenance narration (Option A from report 76, realizes l3k4, report-75 framing) and keeps the durable existence-event decision, the message/router distinction, and the separation rationale (SO_PEERCRED cannot move into router).

condense.new_desc: Streaming is fully schema-derived, not poll-as-permanent and not a lojix-only carve-out: schema-next expresses an event/stream root with opens and belongs relations; schema-rust-next emits the event frame reaching signal-frame's StreamingFrameBody and ObservableSet publish-subscribe machinery; the triad-runtime runner gains a push action and subscriber registry. It is a shared-engine platform project, made cheaper because signal-frame already carries the streaming substrate. Until it lands, handshake-plus-poll suffices and does not block cutover.
condense.reason: Strips the trailing effort-estimate open question (whether the substrate was deliberate scaffolding or leftover) and repetition; keeps the full schema-derived streaming decision, the complete mechanism, the shared-engine framing, and the handshake-plus-poll fallback that doesn't block cutover.

agglomerations.unified_desc: Intent capture is an argued case judged by a guardian, modeled on a court of law: the submitting agent is an advocate; the justification is the strongly-typed argued case (never a stringly blob) carrying typed verbatim psyche testimony (spans surrounded by asterisks) with surrounding context, optional antecedents, and reasoning for why the record and its certainty, domain, kind, and importance are right. The claim is the operation's own Entry, not a justification sub-field; redundant citation-signal tags are excluded, with a cited record relationship carried by the operation or by reasoning prose. The guardian renders one binary yes/no across those dimensions, judging any cross-record operation (e.g. replacing several records with several new ones) as one whole. Certainty is the burden of proof the verbatim quotes must clear on their modal strength: over-claim on hedged words is rejected, the guardian never silently lowers it nor rewrites could into should, so honest understatement is rational and tentative wording stays low and unrewritten; a rejection sends the rewording back to the highest-thinking-power submitting agent. Ship the lean core first (typed verbatim testimony plus a support check, binary verdict); defer the elaborate court structure, typed warrant sub-sections, separate stakes slot, and motions-as-precedent until the core demonstrably falls short.
agglomerations.reason: All seven describe one coherent design: the court-of-law intent-justification protocol. ll41/z3ka/ans1 define the justification and court metaphor; 7xnx the multi-dimension binary judgment; so3b/woku certainty-as-burden-of-proof and modality preservation with the binary gate sending thinking back; bwqe the lean-core-first phasing. CORRECTION over first pass: restored ans1's distinct arrow that redundant citation-signal tags are excluded (cited relationship carried by operation or reasoning prose), which the first-pass unified_desc dropped. Length exceeds the ~280 char guideline because these seven High-importance interlocking records each carry a distinct arrow and intent preservation dominates terseness here.

agglomerations.unified_desc: Root signal shapes begin with an enum (over an 8-bit root) that differentiates input and output spaces by reserving numeric variant ranges, so one first-byte header distinguishes request-like input variants from response-like output variants for the same actor while keeping dispatch cheap.
agglomerations.reason: Both state the identical arrow: the root enum reserves variant-number ranges so the first byte separates input/output spaces. amab is a near-verbatim restatement of 2qia. Confirmed equivalent on re-read.

agglomerations.unified_desc: Parallel daemon versions use versioned sockets and a single discovery mechanism, signal-version-handover (PeerCheck retired): the CLI sends to the next-version daemon, which coordinates back with main; a missing next-version returns an error logged as failed. Upgrade orders that trigger the smart handover arrive through the target component's meta (owner) socket.
agglomerations.reason: All four concern the multi-version daemon dispatch/handover protocol: 7816 the dispatch flow, gq9p the single discovery mechanism (PeerCheck retired), l96d versioned sockets, c2ar the socket the upgrade order arrives on. c2ar's 'owner socket' reconciled to 'meta (owner) socket' per record 3chp's renaming. Every arrow preserved.

condense.new_desc: criome's identity-policy clock is decentralized quorum-attested coarse crystallized-PAST time, not a trusted or precise wall-clock. A time attestation is a forward-extending window, content-addressed by its proposition; a quorum signs that the present falls within it, and it closes at last signature or expiry, becoming a non-forgeable monotonic lower bound on now. Only the past is provable. Every triad input/output and quorum-signed object carries its own AttestedMoment, so time-locks compare against the operation's own proof-of-when; the AttestedMoment is the self-grounding base case carrying no inner moment.
condense.reason: Condensed mini-essay to the operative arrows: crystallized-past coarse quorum time, window mechanics, every object stamped, AttestedMoment self-grounding. Dropped tolerance-gap-widening repetition and worked release-after-T examples.

condense.new_desc: criome is a universal authorization-agreement machine. On admitting a content-addressed object/state transition it emits an authorized-object reference (not the payload); the router, sole matcher for non-direct messages, fans the reference to subscribed components, which fetch the rkyv object via the routing/object-distribution layer. Meaningful objects need quorum authorization, not lone-node signatures. Time-driven pulses are programmed per-contract: an after-time acceptance schedules a later check that, on maturity, re-checks related events and triggers a new quorum-signed acceptance — no ambient heartbeat. criome keeps no operational delivery registry; any local subscription surface is observation/audit only, with the direct criome-to-criome lane the sole non-router path.
condense.reason: Condensed restatement-heavy record to operative arrows: reference-emit + router fan-out, quorum default, programmed time-pulse, no delivery registry. Removed elaboration on lone-signature value and audit-only repetition.

condense.new_desc: criome contracts can escalate a decision to a named adjudicator — mechanical quorum, LLM-assisted panel, smarter agent, or the psyche — whose signed content-addressed verdict criome only verifies. The verdict is a closed typed outcome over an already-submitted object (approve/reject/defer-non-judge-escalate), never a carrier for new content; authoring a new value is a separate editor flow that must admit its own content-addressed object first. Non-judgment is a first-class output and the default LLM adjudicator leans toward abstaining and escalating upward, spending scarce smart-agent and human attention only where needed; the psyche is highest-authority, lowest-availability. EscalateToPsyche stays an inert dead-letter until a psyche-facing UI exists.
condense.reason: Condensed to the adjudicator-ladder arrows; removed repeated vote-on-existing-object explanation and the intent-is-primordial gloss while keeping non-judgment-first-class and the UI-gated dead-letter.

condense.new_desc: criome's internal language is a limited typed policy language over public-key identity atoms (not a general VM), drawing constrained-operation discipline from Ethereum/Tezos/Solana VMs. Public keys are the atomic identity; above them it composes identity contracts from signature quorums (k-of-n) and time-elapsed-varying thresholds, plus explicit divergence-reconciliation objects for network splits (resolution optionally mediated by an LLM-oracle that itself resolves through an identity contract). This is criome's Nexus object and verb vocabulary, to be defined in schema and prototyped.
condense.reason: Condensed to operative arrows: limited typed policy language, pubkey atoms, quorum/timelock contracts, divergence objects, Nexus vocabulary. Kept the LLM-oracle example briefly.

condense.new_desc: CriomOS has two transport lanes. Router is the ordinary fabric for cross-sandbox and cross-network component/object traffic (router-to-router routed delivery, reference push, payload-blind byte move/fetch beyond routing/auth/delivery policy). criome has a direct criome-to-criome peer lane for time-sensitive agreement (quorum signing, crystallized-time windows) carrying only agreement/authorization messages, so criome stays auth/agreement-only and router stays general transport. Each agent runs in its own microVM sandbox; mirror version-controls and moves objects; tailnet gives confidentiality, criome BLS attestations give per-frame authenticity; intra-host sandbox-to-router is tap/L3 (vsock deferred for losing SO_PEERCRED).
condense.reason: Removed the closing restatement ('this realizes router ownership...') and trimmed elaboration while keeping both lanes and the infra facts.

Motivating case: a beautiful Horizon over Nix, Nix being bootstrap infrastructure the future Forge replaces.

agglomerations.unified_desc: A method call on an actor IS the interaction; the mediator pattern survives only as ordinary actor-method-call topology (actors A and B each call a method on mediator actor M whose body decides the outcome), with no Rust trait formalization — the schema-rust composer must not emit Interact<Input> or InteractionActor<Input> traits. What survives from the original interaction model: the two-languages distinction (internal effect vs external wire), effect-table match-driven dispatch as the schema-closing message-to-method mapping, and actor fan-out execution as what methods return.
agglomerations.reason: All three are the same arrow about the interaction/mediator design. 3nla states mediator-as-method-call with no trait, lekz retracts InteractTrait/InteractionActor and forbids composer emission of Interact<Input>/InteractionActor<Input>, ix9y lists the surviving pieces (two-languages, effect-table dispatch, actor fan-out). Verified against file: every distinct arrow is preserved in the union — no decision lost.

agglomerations.unified_desc: Most workspace logic is a MATCH between two domains: create a domain A enum and a domain B enum and let the logic be the match-matrix outcome per cell. Engine operations expose tree-to-tree and enum-to-enum matching surfaces. A cell with no defined behavior returns a typed error or help message (Unavailable etc) — the error surface is part of the trait surface. Generalizes the enum-contact-points pattern (NodeDefinitionShape times NotaValueKind, operator/182) to all engine logic.
agglomerations.reason: u47i (engine ops expose tree-to-tree and enum-to-enum matching surfaces) is the same arrow as 06l6 (logic is matches between two domain enums, error surface part of trait surface). u47i contributes the explicit tree-to-tree surface, preserved in the union; 06l6's typed-error-cell and generalization arrows are all kept.

agglomerations.unified_desc: Design at the post-agent capability frontier toward the ideal end-shape. Agents make previously-impractical engineering routine (zero-downtime complex database migrations, large software-stack rewrites in short time), so for each thing work out the best, most correct, future-oriented, reusable pattern it should be in an ideal world, contrast it explicitly with what exists now so the gap is visible, and target that ideal — reaching for the strongest known ideas rather than a historically-practical compromise.
agglomerations.reason: jys2 (post-agent frontier, target best end-shape, strongest ideas) and cam8 (target the ideal pattern, contrast explicitly with current shape to expose the gap) are the same arrow: ideal/best end-shape, not historical practicality, is the design target. Both jys2's frontier justification and cam8's explicit-gap-contrast method survive in the union.

Type-count: Horizon types do not duplicate across inputs and outputs; reuse the input type as output where it serves rather than defining parallel in/out types.

agglomerations.unified_desc: Agents do not use subagents unless the psyche explicitly authorizes it (belongs in AGENTS.md as a per-keystroke override). Once authorized, dispatch is asynchronous: control returns to the main agent, which keeps working or answers new psyche prompts rather than blocking, and waits only when explicitly asked or when the psyche needs nothing else. Use nonblocking sidecar subagents where they materially reduce audit, checklist, or verification risk while the critical path keeps moving.
agglomerations.reason: All three express one subagent-dispatch policy: 2o3g is the authorization gate (plus its AGENTS.md per-keystroke-override placement), 4728 the async/non-blocking rule (with its wait-only-when-asked nuance), hrh3 the sidecar use once authorized. Each arrow preserved. Dropped the first-pass's invented 'prime designer protocol excepted' clause — no merged record states it. importance lowered to Minimum to match the three Minimum-importance sources rather than inflating to Medium.

agglomerations.unified_desc: On a ratified implementation, operator and designer each write their own implementation from different angles (operator amalgamates existing prototypes; designer iterates forward); comparison is the integration mechanism. Operator always reviews the designer implementation and adopts the better logic, logical planes, and names. Convergence signals the design is settling; divergence surfaces unresolved questions for psyche review, mitigating single-track drift.

agglomerations.unified_desc: When the psyche says refresh intent, it means the agent re-reads recent Spirit intent records to refresh its own context — not edit repo INTENT.md or architecture files unless the psyche explicitly asks for file edits. Refresh intent before maintenance or implementation.

agglomerations.unified_desc: An agent's cryptographic identity IS its Criome master public key: the key is the identifier with no separate UID, and the graduated short forms (~3/~5/~7 chars, full = the key) are methods derived from it. agglomerations.unified_desc: Spirit is an early pilot consumer of Criome-backed operation authorization: Spirit operations needing authentication ask Criome to authorize an exact content-addressed request or log-message digest. Settled envelope: Criome authenticates the SUBMITTER (SO_PEERCRED caller resolved to a registered Criome Identity) via an after-the-fact, non-blocking, out-of-band attestation binding caller to the exact per-operation digest; the Spirit guardian keeps the full content verdict.

agglomerations.reason: 6ry9 says SEMA means database work (the plane that writes durable state) and flags a future .sema rename; 0fdy is that exact rename decision. agglomerations.unified_desc: Version magnitude follows the digits: a code or logic change bumps at least the patch digit (0.3.0 to 0.3.1) so deployed binaries and reports never carry stale versions after behavioral changes; a functionality change such as privacy work bumps the minor digit (0.3 to 0.4). A major first-digit bump toward 1.0 and beyond requires explicit psyche authorization — agents may propose it, the psyche gives the go.

agglomerations.unified_desc: Schema is the engine that turns delimiter-based NOTA into a typed input-output interface specification language: an interface is the input/output for a relationship between actors. The same NOTA text resolves to different meaning under different schemas, and the macro system lets each interaction define its own domain-specific language across components and actors, expressing composable data types and data-driven behavior while preserving quote-free NOTA encoding. Framing: a stricter revival of ASCII's original meaning, representing data as stored, in order.

Dropped uhz9 from the merge: it carries unique load-bearing arrows (import-KIND map import-all/from-git/from-path, schema-as-key-value-struct-at-top, file-splitting, nested sub-engines) the unified wording cannot hold, so uhz9 stays standalone.

agglomerations.unified_desc: The schema language is self-describing: the meta-schema (what a schema IS) is defined in the schema language's own format, and the language is specified by its own schema file. A schema-library repo holds a bare schema file defining the foundational namespace (Schema, Channel, Operation, Reply, Event, Namespace, TypeName, TypeDeclaration, Path) and bootstraps itself with its own basic struct format. The system needs explicit schemas for AssembledSchema data types and the minimal built-in schema object, with a bootstrap layer before schema fully describes itself. The schema-authored self-description is a required tested artifact that lowers through the current engine and exposes the root Schema shape and nested declaration types, even while the bootstrap Rust reader stays hand-written.
agglomerations.reason: khbv (self-describing + foundational namespace), w1ru (specified by own schema file), nz5v (explicit AssembledSchema/built-in schemas + bootstrap layer), z1yz (self-description as required tested artifact through the engine) are facets of one self-hosting decision; unified wording keeps each distinct detail. Corrected importance to Minimum: all four source records are importance=Minimum (proposal's Medium was unsupported).

agglomerations.unified_desc: Each of the three schema types (Signal, Nexus, SEMA) is its own language sharing the SAME 4-position structural shape (Imports, Input, Output, Namespace) and the SAME import/export mechanism: foreign types named via a single-colon namespace path (e.g. signal-frame:Frame, schema:spirit:Entry) mirroring Rust modules with one colon instead of two. They differ only in the signal/nexus/sema-specific types and macros at each position. The namespace position is both the export surface and the definitions surface, with conflict checking across imported exports. Reflect this symmetric input/output/import/export shape for all three in designer reports and per-repo INTENT.md.
agglomerations.reason: 2v9u and rjml both state the three schemas share a 4-position shape and single-colon import/export mirroring Rust modules; 3c0k states imports/exports are part of root structure with conflict checking. One arrow: uniform symmetric import/export root shape across Signal/Nexus/SEMA. Preserves colon-path detail, namespace-as-export-and-definitions, conflict checking. rjml's operator-219-report-gap note dropped as task/report state, acceptable in a Clarification merge.

condense.new_desc: Everything is a struct: a unit variant is a zero-field struct, a data-carrying variant a one-field struct, an enum a single-field struct holding the active variant. A schema node is a tagged variant — a paren whose first object is the tag and second is the carried data — read against the KNOWN type expected at its position, so the tag picks the variant with no sigil. A macro is a Rust struct defining its arguments; the macro-space is a tagged union of those structs; invoking a macro is a tagged variant read against the known macro/reference type, again sigil-free. Positional typing (every position read against the schema-of-schemas) dissolves the enum-versus-macro ambiguity. Supersedes the at-sigil macro invocation of record 1087.
condense.reason: Mini-essay with restatement and an implementation-focus tail ('represent the assembled schema... first so we know how to compose a macro' — task state). Condensed to the operative model: everything-is-struct, sigil-free positional typing, macro-as-struct, supersession of 1087. Importance stays High.

condense.new_desc: The schema language is one recursive shape: a root struct (the dialect-level Schema type) with positional fields of known types, macro-expanded down to scalar leaves. Vector fields carry variable-count macro-call instances; macros are structural expansion by position; inner struct/enum declarations are macros firing at their positions; objects inside a macro call match its pattern; enum bodies re-parse because the enum name carries forward. Recursion terminates at built-in macros expanding leaf structs/enums of scalar types (vectors of integers, vectors of unit-variant enums, booleans, integers, strings). Strings are typed newtypes around character sequences for identity. Position determines type, macros do structural expansion, leaves are scalars.
condense.reason: One long run-on with repeated restatement of 'position determines type'. Condensed to the operative recursive model, removing repetition while keeping every structural rule.

condense.new_desc: The schema substrate is the second iteration of structural insights established first in the psyche's Aski language work (aski-core rkyv contract types for the askic-veric-semac triad, vscode-aski tooling, parse-tree primitives). Those insights — deep typed trees, closed variants, recursive composition, separation of syntax/verification/semantics — crystallized in Aski before the schema engine. Record 656's 'in ASCII' is a speech-to-text corruption of 'in Aski'; correct /341 §1, /344 Slide 2, and any 'ASCII sketches' references to name Aski.

condense.new_desc: Root struct positional fields are bare (no Name@ prefix) — empty-brace imports, [Record@Entry Observe@Query ...] input variants, [RecordAccepted@... ...] output variants, brace-namespace declarations. The Name@ delimiter rule (record 1216) applies to DECLARATIONS inventing a new type name, not to positional values at the root struct's known fields; top-level Input/Output are positional values like imports and namespace. Additional plane roots declared inside the namespace (NexusInput, NexusOutput, SemaInput, SemaOutput) keep Name@ because they are user declarations. Corrects the 1216 carve-out.
condense.reason: Long with restatement ('Same treatment bare {} ... need the same treatment because they are the same kind of thing') and provenance tail. Condensed to the operative rule: positional root fields bare, declarations carry Name@, with the corrected 1216 carve-out.

condense.new_desc: An explicit discriminator on the paren head distinguishes head-applied collection macros (e.g. Vec T) from two-variant enums, since both are structurally identical paren objects at the same node position and the head symbol alone does not mark operator vs variant. Struct brackets and map braces lack this ambiguity — only the paren is doubly loaded. Open: which discriminator (reserved operator-word set, head sigil, or moving collections to suffix sigils/brace so paren stays pure enum) and whether enum is the bare-paren default.

condense.new_desc: Signal schemas are defined in NOTA itself: a NOTA schema language serves as both documentation and source of truth from which Rust code is generated, the way a Rust macro generates types. Direction firm at Medium certainty; exact schema-language shape needs back-and-forth design.

agglomerations.unified_desc: The three NOTA delimiters map structurally to the three schema sections: parentheses hold enum/variant header forms (user-facing operations and variant choices, and generic enum fields use parentheses rather than vector brackets); square brackets hold positional struct field vectors (the body where new types, macro types included, are defined); curly braces hold the name-value namespace of type names to definitions. Schema header root endpoints are parentheses because they are enum choices; the correspondence applies globally.
agglomerations.reason: All four state the one delimiter-to-section correspondence (parens=enum/variant, brackets=struct fields, braces=namespace). lqvx and ogod are strict subsets of 6oun; jqvu is the paren=enum rule applied to generic enum fields, and its distinct 'not vector brackets' arrow is preserved inline. qw1j (the [] is-always-a-vector anti-redefinition arrow) carries a different boundary and is kept separate. Certainty set to Medium (the floor across the merged set; only 6oun was Principle/Medium, others Correction/Medium and Low for jqvu).
