condense.new_desc: Everything in the schema is a serializable data object, the MACRO most of all: a macro is a DATA OBJECT with a name, position, pattern, and template, where pattern and template are themselves data trees (not text with placeholder sigils). Macros are assembled first into assembled-schema data, then read to obtain the usable macro, so they can be pre-assembled, serialized, and loaded as data. Reading any schema loads the pre-assembled macro data and interprets schema-data against it. The only code is one tiny generic interpreter over data; no per-macro bespoke parsing. Every thing round-trips through NOTA text and rkyv bytes, macros included. Forbidden: macro expansion that builds a string from the template and re-parses it (parse-then-black-magic).
condense.reason: Strips the rhetoric (stated with maximum force, test of understanding, slop the psyche rejects). Keeps the full positive arrow AND restores the load-bearing anti-pattern boundary (string-rebuild-and-reparse is forbidden) which the first-pass cut as mere rhetoric — it is a concrete Correction arrow.

condense.new_desc: The structural macro node is a NOTA enum decoded by SHAPE, not by a data tag: decode is TYPE-DIRECTED, performing a structural match on each variant in DECLARATION ORDER (first match wins), then decoding that variant's data recursively (a variant may hold further structural macro nodes); encode emits a NOTA block matching the chosen variant shape (bidirectional). Realized as #[derive(StructuralMacroNode)] on the enum with per-variant shape attributes. Consolidates ejvc + i0e6. Per psyche 2026-06-04/06-05.
condense.reason: Removes the negation aside (NOT a runtime registry/capture-map/string dispatch — covered by the retained 'decoded by SHAPE, not by a data tag') and the meta-narration (never implemented). Keeps type-directed/shape decode, declaration-order match, recursion, bidirectionality, derive.

A structural macro defined in NOTA data form (macros-as-data, Spirit 1263) lowers a bare symbol to a unit EnumVariant and a (Name Type) record to a data EnumVariant with Some(payload). This retires the at-suffix sigil form (VariantName@ PayloadType inside a vector), which placed two interleaved objects per entry and broke vector-as-homogeneous. Input/output enum bodies form the schema signature header. Assembled-schema form is unchanged.
condense.reason: Condenses the mini-essay but RESTORES the retirement arrow the first-pass dropped: the at-suffix sigil-in-vector form is retired for being structurally dishonest. That retirement is a load-bearing Decision (and distinct from dqmc's affirmed @-prefix sugar at struct/enum positions). Keeps the homogeneous-vector rule, two variant forms, macro lowering, unchanged assembled form.

condense.new_desc: Macros ARE sugar syntax in the schema layer and are the universal extension mechanism: the brace-enum form {Variant Payload ...} is a macro expanding to the canonical paren-list ((Variant Payload) ...); both lower to the same Asschema. A macro's shape is two-position — first object is the PATTERN it matches, second is the TEMPLATE for expansion. The engine matches on multiple criteria (delimiter type, internal shape, root-object count, symbol qualification, and combinations), dispatching on the most specific match, and macros fire at the positions they are specified for.
condense.reason: Trims repetition and restatement (TWO equivalent ways..., not adhoc engine code) into the terse operative arrow: sugar=extension mechanism, brace=>paren expansion, pattern/template two-position shape, multi-criteria most-specific dispatch. No arrow lost.

Imports and re-exports resolve through typed import variants (import all, from path, from repository root, adjacent directory, git); a namespace import may carry an optional selection.

Verified each merged record carries no arrow lost in the merge.

Verified operative arrows preserved.

condense.new_desc: Schema is the stable formalization of the architecture the workspace was already reaching for in earlier ASCII work, crystallized (not newly invented) by the component+database detour that revealed the substrate need. One .schema file serves simultaneously as self-describing code documentation and as the wire format, generating both the code and the wire shape.

agglomerations.unified_desc: In a schema a PascalCase (capital-first) name declares or references a TYPE; a camelCase (lowercase-first) name binds a FIELD for code access. There is no explicit field-name syntax: a field name derives by lowercasing its PascalCase type, so a declaration is a bare list of type names (Entry (Topic Kind Summary Context Certainty Quote)) — reversing operator/180's added SchemaField name+type. A PascalCase type may be declared inline at first use and reused by name; resolution is declare-before-use, left to right, enabling single-pass parsing. A type becomes library/export visible by being a top-level namespace entry; the namespace is conceptually the struct of declared types, while enums are category divisions with payload carried by variants. A field name divergent from its type comes from a newtype giving the type its own name ((Certainty (Magnitude)) used as (Certainty)) — no per-position override. NOTA records carry no field names; schema supplies meaning through named type boundaries.
agglomerations.reason: All five state one coherent rule: PascalCase=type / camelCase=field, field names derived by lowercasing PascalCase type names (no explicit field syntax), divergent names via newtype, inline reusable declarations, export via top-level namespace. Restored two arrows the first pass dropped: 4fwy's reversal of operator/180's SchemaField, and 4idp's namespace-is-struct / enum-is-category-division. mchq's no-per-position-override preserved.

agglomerations.unified_desc: The schema root plane surface is a single data-carrying enum named Plane with Signal, Nexus, and Sema variants that carry the actual plane messages, so runtime code matches directly on the plane and engine trait signatures type-check on plane membership — a signal-plane message cannot be passed where a nexus or sema message is expected. This adds plane-membership type-enforcement on top of the three-engine trait-ordering (records 1028/1036), making the trait chain impossible to mis-wire on either axis, and builds on the three schema types as planes (records 964/982). It supersedes the earlier per-plane envelope shape: a thin kind tag paired with a separate envelope is rejected in favor of the single Plane enum.
agglomerations.reason: Both records target one arrow: making plane membership type-enforced so engine trait signatures cannot be mis-wired across planes. qvod proposed per-plane envelope wrappers; ugig supersedes that with a single data-carrying Plane enum, explicitly preferring it over kind-tag-plus-envelope. ugig is the resolution of qvod. Restored the 964/982 cross-reference the first pass dropped. Note: this is a supersession folded into one live decision, not a merge of equals.

condense.new_desc: Pipe-brace traits and impls are data: a catalog referencing impls and methods already existing on the Rust side so schema can name them and method-calling syntax can invoke them from schema-authored bodies. An impl fuses onto its target type as one combined object — a type declaration carries a trailing pipe-brace impl block with the target implicit, introducing a type and its trait participations and method signatures together with no separate marker objects. Each impl-block entry is a bare trait atom (marker impl), a trait atom followed by a bracket of method signatures (body-bearing trait impl), or a bare method signature (inherent method); a method signature is name then a brace parameter struct then a return type, the same shape as a Work-frame leg, naming a referenced existing impl rather than a generated body. Method calls resolve from receiver schema shape plus this fused impl catalog. Generating bodies from schema stays reserved for the narrow mechanical family; deref-by-default is not a goal (it was only scaffolding for impl syntax).
condense.reason: Strips the one rhetorical aside ('because separate objects add too much association complexity') while keeping every operative arrow. Restored the deref-by-default-is-not-a-goal note the first pass dropped — that is an operative negative decision (deref-by-default abandoned), not pure narration.

removals.reason: 'PascalCase booleans are unit variants' is a one-line fragment fully carried by lmzl, which states Bool with True/False PascalCase is conceptually a two-unit-variant enum and explains why NOTA capitalizes them. Redundant; nominate (recoverable) since lmzl covers the arrow.

agglomerations.unified_desc: Tests prove real behavior, not appearance: every pipeline step is genuinely implemented end-to-end and does its real job (green tests are not correctness; design-implementation avoidance is forbidden). Audit existing tests for genuineness, treat in-process synthetic-state tests as lesser proof than real-binary-via-Nix tests, and replace pretend-tests with full-stack Nix integration tests. Every agent test-claim is verifiable against a concrete runnable file path plus its invocation plus witness output; the workspace periodically audits whether claims match reality.
agglomerations.reason: t7h0, fvf1, k9fs all carry the arrow 'tests must exercise the genuine path and be auditable/verifiable, not pretend': t7h0 gives the correctness definition, fvf1 gives the audit-and-replace-with-Nix-integration discipline plus the real-binary-over-synthetic preference, k9fs gives the claim-verifiability rule. Excluded dqg3 from the first-pass merge: it is a distinct Principle (build the missing infrastructure IN the test rather than reporting a blocker; tests are where you do whatever you need) whose exploratory arrow is weakened by folding into a prove-not-pretend Constraint. Left dqg3 untouched. Each affirmative clause of the three merged records is preserved.

agglomerations.unified_desc: Integration tests exercise the actual execution chain in the runtime's own schema-type vocabulary: each engine step takes and returns its plane's schema-object type (signal-engine Signal->Signal, nexus-engine Nexus->Nexus plus Signal-to-Nexus/Nexus-to-SEMA translations, sema-engine SEMA->SEMA), engines are invoked through their trait-surface methods, and assertions are on typed schema-emitted outputs. A passing trace witnesses that Signal, Nexus, and SEMA actor boundaries were entered through their interface methods at runtime, using typed events rather than stringly-typed shortcut state, encoded tokens, or mere source-presence of names.
agglomerations.reason: z2cr gives the per-plane typed-value rule, js3u the no-bypass-trait-surface / no-stringly-typed-state / no-token-collapse rule, 4wdp the runtime-boundary-entry trace (not source-string presence). Same arrow at three granularities; every distinct clause preserved.

agglomerations.unified_desc: Canonical schema/pipeline integration test, file-based end-to-end: real .schema files in real project paths (with local relative imports) drive Nix code generation; Nix builds the spirit CLI and daemon; the CLI sends real rkyv signal frames over a real Unix socket; the Nexus executor (own typed input/output) processes and returns; SEMA holds durable state; the reply travels the whole chain. Each mandatory step is exercised against the real artifact: NOTA structural parse, the single shared codec in nota-next, schema read against the schema-of-schemas, macro/declaration assembly into macro-free Asschema data, the roots model, Rust emission over the shared codec, and the actor system running full constraints on spirit-next.
agglomerations.reason: k9kl gives the full chain shape, qem1 enumerates the mandatory steps proven by real file-based tests, 0ofm gives the .schema-with-local-relative-imports clause (a property of the schema fixtures in that same chain). Closely-coupled single test shape; every step survives. Pulled ddtj and qga2 OUT of the first-pass 5-way merge: ddtj is a distinct fixture-quality constraint (every .schema fixture must be NOTA-legal and parsable by nota-next before lowering — a different arrow than the chain shape), and qga2 is about deliverable form (working prototype over design prose), not test-chain shape. Left both untouched.

agglomerations.unified_desc: VM and nixosTest compute runs on a node carrying the typed VM-testing service and VmHost KVM-hosting capability; the runner is selected by that service capability, never by whichever machine an agent happens to be on. Agents verify the VM-testing host boundary before invoking qemu-backed NixOS VM checks (or ask for the correct host), and never spawn QEMU or a nixosTest on an interactive workstation or any node lacking the service; ordinary nodes remain compile and evaluation surfaces. This homes heavy test-VM compute on the designated VmHost (aligned with cncj/77ic on prometheus), generalizing heavy-work-placement rule 0a9p from AI model work to VM testing.
agglomerations.reason: qnf8 and g7yd state the identical rule: qemu/nixosTest only on the capability-bearing VM-testing host, never on an arbitrary/interactive node. g7yd adds the VmHost service-selection, the never-on-workstation clause, and the 0a9p generalization; both arrows preserved. Conservative two-record merge.

negatives.new_desc: Implementations and tests exercise the real path: the one shared codec (not a per-file emitted reader), genuinely-present features, and macros validated through their real expansion rather than text-round-trip black-boxes. Golden tests assert against an independent expected artifact, not the engine's own re-read output. A finished feature is wired end-to-end, not a stub or placeholder dressed as done.
negatives.reason: Original is a forbidden-stand-ins list (definition-by-negation). Reworded to lead with the affirmative rule (exercise the real path, shared codec, independent golden assertions, real macro expansion); the avoided cases survive only as boundary clauses. Every forbidden item maps to an affirmative requirement, so nothing is lost.

negatives.new_desc: A build or deployment check proves a live architecture or behavior by compiling, executing, round-tripping, or otherwise exercising the real path. Grep is reserved as a narrow negative guard to confirm a retired symbol or forbidden pattern is absent, never as positive proof that something works.
negatives.reason: Primarily a prohibition (positive grep checks not allowed as proof). Reworded to lead with the affirmative rule for what a real check must do; grep's legitimate narrow negative use is preserved.

negatives.new_desc: Across the nota/schema/sema stack, tests load each artifact as a real separate file (.nota, .schema, or .sema) read from disk via a small shared read-fixture-from-file helper, keeping the value-versus-code distinction: the artifact is the real value, the Rust test is the code that loads it. Extends the real-.schema-fixtures direction to the whole stack.
negatives.reason: Operative guidance is framed as a prohibition (tests must NOT inline source as strings). Reworded to lead with the affirmative practice (load real fixture files via a shared helper) while preserving the value-versus-code arrow and the whole-stack extension.

condense.new_desc: Clarifies se72: the lojix cutover e2e deploys a full OS into a throwaway KVM-hosted VM on prometheus (bare-metal AMD-V, live /dev/kvm) via a transient qemu KVM VM under nix, leaving the host's production config and networking untouched. A broken deploy kills only the VM, never the host. No reconfiguring the host generation with microvm.nix/tap/networking.hosts is required; honor 5hir5bnz and do not break Prometheus networking.
condense.reason: Mini-essay with narration of prior designer over-engineering and the 5hir5bnz war story. Condensed to the operative arrows: deploy into a transient KVM VM, leave host untouched, host-untouched does not require host-generation reconfig. 'Clarifies se72' framing preserved.

condense.new_desc: For lojix production-cutover validation, the end-to-end deploy test runs against the full routed microVM target (own Criome domain, reachable IP via the vm-testing module: microvm.nix + tap + projected networking.hosts). Stand it up and prove the lojix daemon deploys a full OS to it and the deploy survives SSH disconnect. The general operator-nspawn e2e pattern btc0 stays available; honor 5hir5bnz and keep Prometheus networking intact.
condense.reason: Long with restatement. Condensed to the operative cutover-validation target while preserving the microVM shape, the SSH-disconnect survival assertion, and the btc0/5hir5bnz boundaries.

condense.new_desc: The CriomOS VM-testing node gains a durable on-demand mode: a persistent VM node defined in cluster data, homed on prometheus, not booted by default but started on demand for continual production-side OS testing on the production host. Prometheus may carry the typed VmHost KVM-hosting capability in its generation while its networking, router, and production function stay untouched; activation uses BootOnce, not a live switch-to-configuration. Its node-service shape follows qkvx as a typed horizon-rs NodeService variant in datom.nota. Extends y1v5, cncj, and se72 toward a durable always-available target.
condense.reason: Mini-essay with heavy cross-reference narration and a superseded-bead war story (primary-wvey). Condensed to the durable-on-demand-node arrow plus the host-scope boundary, BootOnce activation, and typed NodeService requirement.

condense.new_desc: The criome cluster is validated by one reusable interface driving an actually-networked multi-node test substrate with the spirit gate authenticated end-to-end. One identical propagation test targets three substrates: a hermetic host-untouched runNixOSTest VM cluster on prometheus (7let), an opt-in durable on-demand microvm node on prometheus (77ic), and opt-in DigitalOcean on-demand droplets for cross-machine validation. It drives real daemons across separate kernels and asserts the spirit gate accepts an authorized head and rejects an unauthorized one, fail-closed, building from the 1-of-1 local gate (xhwa) toward multi-machine quorum (jk1w).
condense.reason: Long but mostly operative; trimmed restatement and tightened the three-substrate enumeration while preserving every distinct clause (substrates, fail-closed assertion, xhwa->jk1w trajectory).

Nominate (recoverable) is the right level, not a hard remove, since it carries a mild unique watch-for-fake-long-runs arrow.

agglomerations.unified_desc: Every component CLI takes exactly one NOTA argument: an inline NOTA string wrapped in shell double quotes (the default) or a path to a NOTA file. The file-path form is reserved for cases that need it (binary signal-encoded file paths, or NOTA with embedded shell metacharacters too painful to escape inline); multi-line agent-rephrased context fields stay inline. Bracket-string notation lets NOTA itself avoid double quotes so the whole object passes as one double-quoted shell argument. Help retrieval is one NOTA arg too: spirit (Help), spirit (Record Help), spirit (Record (Slot1 (Workspace Help))).
agglomerations.reason: hetk, kg2z, sg4o all express the single-NOTA-argument CLI contract: one NOTA arg as inline-quoted string (default) or file path, the reserved file-path cases, multi-line-stays-inline, shell double-quoting plus bracket-string notation, and help-via-NOTA. All distinct arrows preserved affirmatively; the /312 war-story framing and the bare prohibition wording in hetk dropped in favor of the positive rule. Importance taken from hetk (High).

negatives.new_desc: Spirit ordinary agent-facing replies omit database markers; marker state is returned only from an explicit operation that asks for it.
negatives.reason: Primarily framed as a prohibition. Reworded to lead with the positive rule (ordinary replies omit markers) while preserving the boundary: markers come only from an explicit marker-state operation. Faithful.

condense.new_desc: Operations that extract or emit Spirit content take a customizable output-target enum as the request's final field: Stdout, Stderr, or File with a path payload. Stderr is one normal output option, not an error channel. CollectRemovalCandidates uses it; future export/dump operations reuse it for a uniform extraction wire interface.
condense.reason: Restatement and rationale narration trimmed; the operative arrow plus the enum variants, the Stderr-is-normal clarification, CollectRemovalCandidates, and the reuse intent all preserved.

agglomerations.unified_desc: Valid lane shapes are <role>, second-/third-/fourth-<role> (ordinal prefixes copy the whole following role for parallel capacity), and qualified-prefix-<role> for specialized scope (system-<role>, cluster-<role>, pi-<role>; e.g. cluster-operator is an operator-specialized lane).
agglomerations.reason: pguv (valid lane shapes) and 56i0 (ordinal prefixes copy the whole role; cluster operator is operator-specialized) are the same positive canonical lane-naming arrow. EXCLUDED nytm and lo72 from the merge: each carries a distinct RETIREMENT arrow with a specific old->new mapping (-assistant suffix retired; -specialist retired, system-specialist->system-operator) that the positive-shape wording does not preserve. Those are left as-is.

agglomerations.unified_desc: A dispatched subagent inherits the dispatcher's lane, lock, and report-numbering slot rather than creating its own; the dispatcher assigns each subagent its report number inside the dispatcher's own role report subdirectory, and assigns parallel subagents their report lanes before launch.
agglomerations.reason: xlfo (inherits lane/lock/report-slot, no own lane), ltfr (report number in dispatcher's subdir), and j60y (parallel subagents get report lanes before launch) are facets of one subagent-inheritance/report-lane arrow. EXCLUDED uxsa: it is a distinct Principle with a causal arrow (two subagents CANNOT work the same thing BECAUSE they share the single lock) that the inheritance wording would blur into a weaker 'work on different things'. Left uxsa as-is.

agglomerations.unified_desc: Operators stay current on others' output before acting: they read peer operator reports first and watch new designer reports.
agglomerations.reason: 6i29 (read peer operator reports first) and ruv2 (watch new designer reports) are the same arrow: operators ground in others' reports. Both facets preserved affirmatively; taking Medium (6i29) over VeryLow (ruv2) is fine. No distinct arrow lost.

negatives.new_desc: Report lanes are per-role: each agent writes in its own reports role directory. Cross-lane report writing happens only when the psyche explicitly instructs it for a specific task, never touching that other lane again unless re-instructed; it is occasionally practical but not normalized.
negatives.reason: Constraint led with a prohibition (never write in another lane's directory; not normalized). Reworded to lead affirmatively (write in your own lane) while preserving the boundary: the cross-lane exception is psyche-gated, single-task, reverts, and is explicitly not normalized.

negatives.new_desc: Reports are per-role outputs exempt from the claim flow; editing a role-owned report needs no workspace claim. Claim the workspace only when editing shared coordination surfaces or repo/workspace files outside the reports exemption.
negatives.reason: Correction led with a do-not (do not claim to edit a report). Reworded to state the affirmative rule first (reports are claim-exempt) while preserving the boundary (claim only for shared surfaces). Faithful to original.

Condensed to the operative arrow (prime designer parallel by default; exception scoped to that chair, not other lanes) preserving both the exception and its scope limit.
